For example, Helot can be killed with one shot from Katigul. Or you can delay using her ultimate as much as possible. If she's killed, the team will fall apart

Corpo exalt for 3 barrier
Corpo enhance herself, Arachne 4 cost dupe next skill for corpo, corpo use the posse that give 3 temporary amp
Then she use dmg scaling barrier

Especially if winkle vuln you
can't arachne also just exalt onto the kath to stop that from happening
If arachne get alienus regen enough to turn 2 exalt then she can just exalt helot anyway
Helot will fill her exalt because of the X card dmg
You just equip wheel then spam skill to ramp kath's wheel that dmg scale your exalt
You guys kind of see the problem now?
Yes, but that's the least of my worries. I need to act according to the situation. Usually, I slow down the Corpo or weaken their attack to minimize damage
Then what about helot?
You used X card
what pressure are you using to where the helot user can't just comfortably wait out that weakness turn
So you have 0 arithmetica to use aigis' weakness
if you even get it and can target her

Reminder that they nerfed kath's X card into dealing 8 dmg per arith consumed instead of 10, unless you RNG the wheels that increase skill dmg you won't be able to properly one shot with X card anymore
Also the posse that draw 3 lowest arith card got nerfed into 2 draw
besides if the helot user just gets a good roll then they can just spend the turn fortifying and stacking their deck even more
So you need to already have one of the 3 cards combo in your hand prior to that setup X card
cause off the top of my head I can't think of a single other traphase character who can work that fast without being restricted by auto targetting like sorel
The X card strat of kath could have worked befoee if it wasn't nerfed, but now it need wheel that buff your skill dmg or it's too slow to setup

In my deck, X is the last card used. It takes me about 2-3 turns to kill Gelot, 50% of the time. Before that, I slow the enemy as much as possible. 3-4 arithmetic cards are enough for this, thanks to Arachne's Fate
You need either titan blade or her own SSR wheel, then you need the posse to draw lowest arith card or it's too RNG while also needing 1 of the 3 cards combo prior in your hand
I mean, look
You genuinely need skill buff wheel
Or else it's fked
Before if you didn't had wheel the X card dmg dealt 110 dmg
At 10 arithmetica and you could easily draw those cards because of posse giving you 3 draws
Now it's 90 dmg without wheel
And even the posse give only 2 draws
And it's assuming you manage to 10 arithmetica
Sometimes you don't start 2nd to get the chest that gives 3 arithmetica + 3 draws
Helot hp is 108 hp btw
And you can just use winkle's 1 cost prepare shield card on arachne
So she is labeled as highest hp
You see those problem now?
Arachne's hp is 105 btw
Just 1 selected shield from winkle is needed
If you bring stuff like doll or others
You can just winkle taunt
While using the shield on arachne
<@312050834890293249> overall i'm not trying to be rude about your kath strat, it's just not viable anymore because of prior nerfs and current helot meta is just too cancerous and way too fast
If you can't kill then she would have already regenerated enough alienus to wipe your team
It's that braindead of a team

If I were to describe it step by step, omitting some situational aspects:
To start, there are 3 options for killing Helot:
1) Vulnerability + X card
2) Sorel and AoE damage
3) Arachne Cone and X card.
One of my strategies:
1. Disiere and Oblivion prevent the enemy from building up their ult/buff stacks on Helot. This is a must.
2. Draw Arachne Cone.
3. Even if you don't draw Arachne Cone, you have Sorel.
4. Start preparing as soon as your Sorel charges.
5. On turn 3, use Undying Sun and see what happens. There are two options:
  1) If you draw an X card or memento, you shoot Helot.
  2) If you don't draw any cards, you wait another turn, apply a weakening effect, and wait for turns 4 or 5.
6. Hang a web through Agrippa if the Helot is Chains Unbound.
7. Discharge everything you have; usually, the damage is already enough and the Helot dies.

Optionally, even if Copro uses a 3-barrier strike, it won't hurt as much, and you can breathe a sigh of relief the next turn, as depending on your strategies, you have the option of shooting another target—either Copro or Arachne herself.
Sometimes even a memento allows you to draw another X card on the 4th or 5th turn (again, you were born with a shirt on)

A critical issue: if you know the Helot will buff Corpo next turn, be sure to remove the Helot's attack stacks and slow it down. But usually, this is still a loss—it shouldn't come to that.
Since there's now the card Earth-End, which gives an insane boost to the Helot. <:Helot_slack_off:1459092159465521184>

If two hits come and a +30 card, it'll be ready by turn 2. But I don't need it that early.

Your goal is to ensure it doesn't go to waste—in this case, the target should already be dead after using it.
